title: 10 best herbal essential oils for oily face
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for oily face are natural blends that help control sebum production and reduce oiliness. These oils work by regulating the skin's sebaceous glands, preventing excessive oil production and clogged pores. The best herbal essential oils for oily face include Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Thymus vulgaris, Aloe barbadensis, Pelargonium graveolens, Salvia officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, and Geranium maculatum. If left untreated, oily face can lead to acne, premature aging, and low self-esteem, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which work to balance the skin's pH levels and reduce sebum production. When used in a toner or serum, tea tree oil can help dissolve excess oil and unclog pores, leaving the skin feeling refreshed and matte. Additionally, its ability to regulate the skin's natural moisture barrier helps to prevent future oil buildup, keeping the face looking healthy and radiant.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Kills bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause acne and inflammation, reducing the risk of breakouts and clogged pores.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces redness and swelling associated with acne, rosacea, and other skin conditions, helping to calm and soothe the skin.
Antioxidant property: Neutralizes free radicals and prevents damage caused by environmental stressors and pollution, which can contribute to oily skin and premature aging.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of cineole, which helps to reduce sebum production and combat acne.
Buds: Contain a higher percentage of cineole than leaves, making them effective in treating mild to moderate acne and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when dew is present for maximum oil content and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm shade for 7-10 days or use a food dehydrator on low setting for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 500ml of distilled water at 60°c for 1 hour.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the distillation water using a separator funnel or filter paper.
Bottle and store the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in an amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. When applied topically, it can help to balance the skin's pH levels and reduce excess oil production. The calming and soothing effects of lavender also help to regulate sebum secretion, leaving the skin feeling refreshed and revitalized. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties combat acne-causing bacteria, further reducing the appearance of oily skin. Overall, incorporating English lavender essential oils into your skincare routine can lead to a smoother, more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: English Lavender essential oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, common issues associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, blackheads, and other inflammatory skin conditions often seen in oily skin.

Antioxidant properties: English Lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can cause oxidative damage and contribute to skin issues like premature aging, dullness, and increased oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of English lavender contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in regulating sebum production and reducing redness in oily skin.
Flowers: The flowers of English lavender are rich in linalool, a natural antifungal agent that helps to combat acne-causing bacteria and soothe inflammation on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when dew is still present on leaves.
Dry the harvested flowers for 7 days by spreading them thinly on paper towels or screens in a warm, dry area.
Steam-distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using 1 liter of water and 100 grams of flower material at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Filter the obtained distillate through a coffee filter to separate essential oil from water byproduct in about 30 minutes.
Bottle the obtained lavender essential oil in dark glass bottles, store it in a cool place and use within 6 months due to its shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's oil production. It also possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce the appearance of pores and soothe acne-prone skin. Additionally, rosemary oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals. By incorporating rosemary essential oils into your skincare routine, you can say goodbye to excess oiliness and hello to a healthy, radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Rosemary essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms on the skin, which can contribute to acne and oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Astringent property: Rosemary essential oil has astringent properties that help to constrict pores and reduce their size, which can be beneficial in reducing the appearance of large pores and excess oil production on the face.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties, rosemary leaves help to balance oil production and reduce inflammation in acne-prone skin.
Roots: Rosemary roots are known for their ability to stimulate circulation and reduce excess oil secretion, making them effective in treating oily skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from your garden or purchase high-quality dried rosemary leaves in bulk.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves using a dehydrator or air-dry them for several hours to reduce moisture levels.
Combine 100g of dried rosemary leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to ensure even extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance and tighten the pores. It also has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and inflammation caused by excess oil production. Additionally, sage oil's antibacterial properties help to prevent bacterial growth on the skin, which can contribute to oily skin conditions. As a result, using sage essential oils can help to regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage essential oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts caused by excess sebum production.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage essential oil can help reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, promoting a smoother and more even complexion.

Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to premature aging, leaving the skin looking healthier and more radiant despite its natural oiliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in controlling acne and reducing redness on oily skin.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and purify the skin, making it suitable for oily face care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves at dawn or mid-morning when their oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves in small bunches for 7-10 days in a warm well-ventilated area.
Muddle 200 grams of dried salvia sclarea leaves with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the essential oils.
Strain and filter the resulting liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil production. It also contains antibacterial agents that prevent acne-causing bacteria from thriving, thereby reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily skin. Additionally, thyme oil has antiseptic qualities that help to clean pores deeply, removing impurities and dirt that can clog pores and exacerbate oily skin. By balancing the skin's natural pH levels, thyme essential oil helps to regulate sebum production, leaving the face looking refreshed and healthy.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme essential oil has potent antibacterial properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts.
Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of thyme essential oil help to constrict pores and reduce sebum production, leaving the skin looking smoother and more balanced.

Antioxidant Properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation on the skin, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antiseptic properties, making them effective in controlling acne and reducing inflammation associated with oily skin.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to regulate sebum production, calming the skin and preventing clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and chop them into small pieces for 20 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of chopped thyme leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil and stir for 10 minutes.
Place the mixture in a glass container and store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container for 15 minutes.
Decant the essential oil from the carrier oil and bottle it tightly with a dropper top after 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's natural oil production. It also has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by clogged pores. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting overall skin health and clarity.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: This property helps to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, preventing breakouts and infections that can lead to oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe redness and irritation associated with oily skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Decongestant: The decongestant properties of this essential oil help to reduce excess oil production by opening up pores and promoting a healthy flow of sebum, leaving the skin looking balanced and refreshed.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain antimicrobial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and soothing redness on oily skin.

Roots: The roots of this plant have natural astringent properties, which can help to shrink pores and reduce sebum production, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for oily face care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a pesticide-free environment in early morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm air oven at 40°c for 24 hours or until crumbly.
Grind 10g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Add 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil to the ground herb in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Filter the infused oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth and store in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with oily face because of its unique ability to balance and regulate sebum production. The oil contains natural astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil and tighten pores, leaving skin looking smoother and more refined. Additionally, wild geranium's antiseptic and antibacterial qualities help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily skin. This results in a clearer, healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Wild geranium essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts on oily skin.

Astringent: The astringent property helps to reduce sebum production, balancing the skin's natural oil production and minimizing the appearance of pores, making it an effective treatment for oily faces.

Anti-inflammatory: Wild geranium essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin irritations on oily faces.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce skin inflammation and balance oil production.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, barks have astringent properties that help to tighten pores and control sebum output.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh geranium maculatum leaves from your garden or purchase dried ones.
Dry the harvested leaves for at least two weeks in a warm dry place ensuring even drying.
Place one ounce of dried geranium maculatum leaves in a clean glass jar with an iron-free lid.
Add 12 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil to the jar and stir well for ten minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking occasionally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with oily face because of its unique composition that targets excess oil production. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and linalool, have natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to regulate sebum secretion, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling shine. Additionally, curry plant oil has a balancing effect on the skin's pH, helping to neutralize the skin's natural acidity and reduce inflammation caused by excessive oil production, leaving the skin looking clearer and more even-toned.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Turmeric essential oil's antibacterial properties help control acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores, which in turn helps to reduce excessive oil production.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in turmeric essential oil neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and skin damage, leading to a reduction in sebum production and an even-toned complexion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to oily skin and acne, by regulating the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, curry plant roots help reduce acne and prevent further breakouts on oily skin.
Leaves: With their natural astringent properties, curry plant leaves can help control sebum production and tighten pores, making them an effective choice for oily face care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal oil content retention.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves immediately by gently brushing off dirt particles and placing them in a clean cloth bag.
Steam distill 100g of dried helichrysum italicum leaves with 500ml of distilled water using a copper still for 1-2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Monitor the distillation process and collect the essential oil as it is produced, taking care not to over-distill or under-distill the plant material.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrtle essential oils helps with oily face because of its remarkable properties that help to balance the skin's natural oil production. The antibacterial and antifungal compounds in myrtle essential oils work wonders by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungus that can contribute to excessive oil secretion, thereby minimizing pores and controlling sebum production. Additionally, its astringent properties help to tighten pores, leaving your skin looking refined and radiant, making it an excellent natural remedy for oily skin woes.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Myrtle essential oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, preventing breakouts and inflammation associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory property: Myrtle essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe and calm redness and irritation caused by oily skin, reducing puffiness and minimizing the appearance of pores.

Antioxidant property: Myrtle essential oil is rich in antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from environmental stressors and oxidative damage that can lead to premature aging and clogged pores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, myrtle roots help to regulate sebum production and soothe acne-prone skin.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, myrtle barks possess antibacterial properties that combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation on oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh myrtus communis leaves and stems from a pesticide-free area in the morning.
Clean and dry the plant material by gently brushing off any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the plant material using 10 grams of fresh material per 100 milliliters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a paper filter or coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the myrtus communis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. It effectively controls excess oil production, reduces pores, and purifies the skin. The citrusy scent also has a clarifying effect, lifting dirt and impurities without stripping the skin of its natural moisture. remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass oil's antibacterial properties help combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm redness and irritation associated with oily skin, promoting a more balanced complexion.
Astringent properties: Lemongrass oil's astringent properties help to shrink pores and tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles and controlling excess sebum production.
remedy_parts: Stems: Stems are commonly used to extract lemongrass oil, which has natural astringent and antifungal properties that help control sebum production and reduce acne in oily skin.
Roots: Lemongrass roots are also used to make essential oils that help balance the skin's pH levels, reducing excess oil production and inflammation associated with acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from a sustainable source, weighing approximately 20 kilograms for extraction.
Clean and wash the harvested leaves in cold running water to remove any impurities or debris.
Steam-distill the washed cymbopogon citratus leaves using a high-quality distillation apparatus with a condenser at 100 degrees celsius for two hours.
Collect and separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol, filtering it through a coffee filter or paper to remove any particles.
Store the final cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, kept away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to one year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily face with herbal essential oils is by blending Tea Tree oil, known for its antibacterial properties, with Geranium oil, which helps balance hormones that contribute to oil production. Apply a few drops of the blend to a cotton pad and gently sweep across the face to reduce sebum secretion and shrink pores. This natural combination also soothes and calms the skin, leaving it refreshed and balanced.
